object_under_evaluation: restart fidelity of checkpoint-based long-horizon inquiry routes
claim_boundary: On this bounded checkpoint-and-relaunch family, the route can often preserve its active axis and open contradictions honestly, but that support weakens when delta boundaries blur or when relaunch still needs hidden transcript continuity.
limitations: This report does not prove final inquiry correctness.
This report does not prove broad long-horizon capability.
This report does not replace witness-trace or return-anchor evals.
strongest_support_signal: The checkpoint preserved objective, active axis, open questions, and next tests clearly enough for a bounded relaunch.
strongest_restart_gap: Delta separation weakened because canon and memory updates became easier to read together than apart, which raised false-continuity pressure.

case_notes.checkpoint_note: The checkpoint named the current thesis, open contradictions, and next tests without pretending the inquiry had already converged.
case_notes.delta_note: memory_delta and canon_delta were both present, but the canon update summary looked cleaner than the evidence split justified.
case_notes.continuity_note: The relaunch stayed mostly inside the checkpoint pack, yet one restart note still hinted at unseen transcript continuity.
